Contact Rare & Manuscript Collections

Clarence Stein papers, 1905-1983.
Collection Number: 3600

Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Cornell University Library

DESCRIPTIVE SUMMARY

Title:
Clarence Stein papers, 1905-1983.
Repository:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Collection Number:
3600
Abstract:
Contains job files relating to Clarence Stein's work as an architect and city planner, along with writings, including material pertaining to his book, Toward New Towns for America. Also contains personal and professional correspondence; correspondence and photographs pertaining to travels in China and the Far East; photographs of Stein, friends, and family; and medals, certificates, and pins.
Creator:
Stein, Clarence S.
Stein, Clarence S. (Title of work: Toward new towns for America..)
Quanitities:
31.4 cubic feet.
Language:
Collection material in English
BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE

Architect, city planner (lived 1882-1975).
Clarence S. Stein began his career as an architect, but turned his attention to planning by the early 1920s. From 1923 to 1926 he was chairman of the New York State Housing and Regional Planning Commission and was among the founders of the Regional Plan Association in 1923. With his partner Henry Wright, he was a leading proponent of the "garden city" concept of planning. He designed or participated in the design of Sunnyside Gardens, Queens, New York; Radburn, New Jersey; Greenbelt, Maryland; Greendale, Wisconsin; Greenhills, Ohio; and Baldwin Hills Village, Los Angeles, California. Stein was awarded the Gold Medal of the American Institute of Architects (1956), the Distinguished Service Award of the American Institute of Planners (1958), and the Ebenezer Howard Memorial Medal, honoring the British advocate of garden cities.
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION

Includes job files, proposed jobs, writings and speeches, general files, research materials, and reprints and articles. Job files record Stein's involvement in the design of public buildings, places of worship, museums, and homes; his time as secretary of the Housing Committee of the New York State Reconstruction Commission under Governor Alfred W. Smith; and material on Radburn, New Jersey; Kitimat, British Columbia; Sunnyside Gardens, New York; Hillside Homes, Bronx, New York; and Chatham Village, Pittsburgh, Pennsylvania. Proposed job files include plans and designs for jobs which never materialized. Writings include material related to the publication of his book, Toward New Towns for America, including photographs, plans, and maps used in the book. Also includes copies of articles and speeches by Stein. General files include unpublished writings; reports on greenbelt towns; material on the Regional Plan Association of America and its successor, the Regional Development Council of America; and community planning and cost studies. Correspondence files include personal and professional correspondence with other planners, including correspondence with the American Institute of Architects (AIA), especially Carl Feiss of that organization; Canadian planners working on Metropolitan Toronto; English planners; personal friends such as Benton MacKaye, Albert Mayer, and Lewis Mumford; and his wife, Aline MacMahon Stein.
Also, correspondence and photographs pertaining to travels in China and the Far East. Reprints and articles include writings of other planners including Frederick Ackerman, Catherine Bauer Wurster, Russell Van Nest Black, Henry Churchill, Vernone De Mars, Arthur Glickson, Eugene Klaber, Benton MacKaye, Albert Mayer, Lewis Mumford, and Henry Wright. Collection also includes photographs and negatives, slides, plans, and maps of much of Stein's major work; and material concerning the work of Marjorie Sewell Cautley (Cornell University Class of 1917), landscape architect for Stein's projects at Radburn, New Jersey and Hillside Homes in the Bronx, New York. Photographs of Stein, friends, and family, and candid and publicity photographs of Aline MacMahon Stein. Medals, certificates, and pins, including Stein's gold medal from the American Institute of Architects, are also included.
Also, a videotape of "Baldwin Hills Village: 'A Village in a City,' " 1942-1950, by Reginald D. Johnson, copied from the 16mm color original film (28 min.).
Plaques and phonograph recordings.
RELATED MATERIALS

For some films of Radburn and Sunnyside, see the Marjorie Sewell Cautley papers, #4908, http://resolver.library.cornell.edu/cgi-bin/EADresolver?id=RMM04908.
See also the Milton Glass Kitimat files, #6896, http://resolver.library.cornell.edu/cgi-bin/EADresolver?id=RMM06896
INFORMATION FOR USERS
Cite As:

Clarence Stein papers, #3600. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

Slum clearance, Robert Kohn & federal housing, Hillside approval July 1933

L. Mumford writing for "New Republic" on the failure of government housing policy Nov. 1933

Hillside Dec. 1933

Hillside site, Housing Authority bill passed, slum clearance Jan. -Feb. 1934

R. Kohn A. I. A. gold medal, Hillside Apr. 1934
Box 34 Folder 9
R. Kohn leaving housing post, concern about Hillside Project June 1934
Box 34 Folder 10
House plan sketched for Arthur and Lillie, Wichita Museum work, bricks for Hillside, "Good Earth" as possible film for Aline July 1934
Box 34 Folder 11
Mumford, Hillside Aug. 1934
Box 34 Folder 12
Mumford as cook, Fieldston School, N. Strauss resigns N.R.A. to work extensively with Hillside Project Sept. & Nov. 1934

Hillside, Jack Berstein's death Dec. 1934
Box 35 Folder 1
Wichita Museum, use of concrete, Hillside Homes, City planning taught as part of architecture, Kuzun manager of Hillside Jan. 1935
Box 35 Folder 2
Wichita Museum, Phipps II, Landscaping for Hillside, A. Mayer, L. Mumford, Henry Wright, Henry Klaber, Lee Laurie' s Sculpture for Wichita Museum, Aline's screen test for "The Good Earth" Feb. 1935
Box 35 Folder 3
Valley Stream, Long Island, Jews in Germany, New Deal and Relief, Eken taking credit for Hillside, Tugwell not interested in purchasing land for Valley Stream, California housing proposal, museum convention in Washington, Hillside streets May 1935
Box 35 Folder 4
Outlook for Federal Housing Authority bleak according to F. Ackerman, F.D.R., Valley Stream Project, hope for government housing fading, N.Y.C. mayor June 1-19, 1935
Box 35 Folder 5
Nagouchi bust of Aline, Hillside opening, conversation with N.Y. City's mayor, Wichita Museum visit, decisions on color June 20-30, 1935
Box 35 Folder 6
Hillside plantings, fantasy travel sketches, description of "The Normandie," a cruise ship visit to Clarence Wollett's estate, Clarence Stein celebrates his birthday. Aline 's decisions on film career July 2-18, 1935
Box 35 Folder 7
Red Hook project, working on slum clearance, Ely Kahn's book, Aline Bernstein's attempted suicide July 19-31, 1935
Box 35 Folder 8
Sketches for Princeton Museum described, Catherine Bauer, Phipps Apartments, Tugwell, bids for Hillside approved in Washington, landscape work at Hillside, working with Tugwell's housing committee September 1935
Box 35 Folder 9
Gertrude Stein's house, Wichita Museum well received, Museum book, attitude toward communism, Tugwell, Catherine Bauer, Princeton Museum, Hillside, Albert Mayer, current movie October 1-18, 1935
Box 35 Folder 10
Wichita Museum opens, site in N.J. for another Garden City. Henry Wright visits October 19-31, 1935
Box 35 Folder 11
Plans for trip to the Orient, possible roles for Aline in Hollywood November 1935
Box 35 Folder 12
'Thousand Years,' Fred Ackerman's housing authority group view models of housing developments, comment about the height of buildings at Rockefeller Center, Baldwin Hills plans Sept. -Oct. 1936
Box 35 Folder 13
Works on plans for World's Fair; finishing sister's house; fields for schools, auditorium; costume museum plans with Aline Berstein; speculates on future Radburns and Chatham Villages; F. Bigger's Views of Resettlement Division Nov. 1-Dec. 2 1936
Box 35 Folder 14
World's Fair, Costume Museum Dec. 31 , 1936
Box 35 Folder 15
Letters from "Silver Hill", "housers" meeting in Cleveland with Klaber, Feiss, Churchill April 1 -Nov. 1937
Box 35 Folder 16
Modern Art Museum; on Strauss speaking at the Architectural League; the film "The River" encourages work on a film of Federated Cities or City Planning; World's Fair plans; possibility of housing group to be planned for Vassar; meets with Sir Raymond Unwin; meeting with Nelson Rockefeller on Modern Museum plans; lectures at "Boston Tech"; plans for new house at "A Thousand Years" Jan . --April 1938
Box 35 Folder 17
May-Sept. 1938
Box 35 Folder 18
Father's death, March 27;' John Elliot presides at memorial service; work on museum competition [Springfield]; visit to Radburn March-Sept. 1938
Box 35 Folder 19
Letters from A Thousand Years; drawings on letters; conversation concerning world politics with Lewis Mumford, Frederick Lee and Benton MacKaye 1940
Box 36 Folder 1
"job" with Navy Dept.; Speaker/Dinner with Mrs. Roosevelt; Asked to be consultant to U.S. Housing Authority; Indian Head Development/Defense Housing; Meetings with John Carmody, Chief of U.S.H.A.; Herbert Emmerrich/new job with President; Committee of Housing "folk" - revise National Program; East Side Development - conversation with Mayor; Clarence Stein medal Jan. /Feb. 1941
Box 36 Folder 2
Ernest Grunsfield/Ascoli House; Discussion with Borough Pres . E. River Dr. site for Negro Housing; Meeting - Kenneth Reid, editor Pencil Points; Pittsburgh Defense Housing project; Indian Head; Roland Wank/Chief Architect T.V.A. March/April 1941
Box 36 Folder 3
Pittsburgh Defense Housing; Chatham Village visit; Gramercy Park House; Attends opening Fort Greene Housing; Banquet for Francis Taylor, May 1941
Box 36 Folder 4
Ralph Griswald, landscape architect for Pittsburgh projects; Indian Head supervision; Stowe Twp. /Pittsburgh project; Stotz brothers/Pittsburgh Associates; John Carmody postpones last Pittsburgh project/Clareton, June/July 1941
Box 36 Folder 5
Coal mines under one Pittsburgh project /rearrange site plan/Shaler; Indian Head supervision; Pittsburgh/Clareton drawings approved though not to be built at once; Trip to N.W. [Portland/Spokane], Ben Kizer, lawyer/"leader" of regional planning in N.W., Aug. -Oct. 1941
Box 36 Folder 6
Federal Housing Program; Architectural League dinner with Hugh Ferris, Albert Kahn & Roland Wank, T.V.A; Talk with Herbert Emmerich; Talk with John Edelman on getting organized labor behind housing, Jan. -April, 1942
Box 36 Folder 7
Herbert Emmerich/community facilities for war communities; Community requirements for dormitory groups; Next jobs - trailer camps to isolated family communities, May 1942
Box 36 Folder 8
Setting standards for war housing; Letters from Silver Hill, New Canaan, Conn., June-Sept. 1942
Box 36 Folder 9
Letters from Silver Hill; Income schedule 1942; War saving bonds; Budget for Nov., Dec. & Jan., Oct.-Dec. 1942
Box 36 Folder 10
Letter from Double U Ranch, Tucson , Arizona Jan. -March 1943
Box 36 Folder 11
Misc. [1] 1944
Box 36 Folder 12
Letters from Neurological Institute, N.Y.C.; Letters from Silver Hill July 1945
Box 36 Folder 13
Letters from Silver Hill August 1945
Box 37 Folder 1
Radburn & 3 Greenbelts; Meeting with association of younger planners of N.Y. region & Hugh Pomeroy, Director Westchester Planning Board; Sage Foundation report on Greenbelts; 10th anniversary of Greenbelt; Writing "The story of Sunnyside", Jan. -Sept. 1947
Box 37 Folder 2
Writing about Radburn; A. I. A. Committee visits municipal housing developments; Trying to find out who was responsible for order to sell out the Greenbelts to highest bidder; Met with students from Columbia's Planning Dept., Jan. -July 1948 19
Box 37 Folder 3
R.P.C.A. meeting in N.Y.C.; Matthew Nowicki & California Markets; Present Columbus Circle solution to Citizens ' Union Committee on Planning, Oct. -Dec. 1948
Box 37 Folder 4
Columbus Circle; R.P.C.A. changed to Regional Planning Development Council Jan. 1949
Box 37 Folder 5
Kitimat sketch & letter; Misc. 1951 1952
Box 37 Folder 6
"Alcan job"; January Harper's story on Kitimat; Treatment with Dr. Zabriskie; Note to Aline from Lewis Mumford; Resign Kitimat job, Clarence Stein; Resign Alcan job, Clarence Stein, copy of that letter; Memo to Albert Mayer - Town Planning Review crisis - Western Australia job; Supporting Town Planning Review; Wants to write another book on "The Regional City", Jan. -Feb. 1953
Box 37 Folder 7
Misc. Sept. -Oct. 1953
Box 37 Folder 8
Co-op Housing Report Oct. 1954
Box 37 Folder 9
Visit Pratt Institute Dept. of Architecture; Speaks of Guggenheim, Nov. -Dec. 1959
Box 37 Folder 10
Misc., May 1966
Box 37 Folder 11

Box 37 Folder 12
Miscellaneous, undated

Mayer, Arthur L. "Myths and Movies'" 1951
Box 18 Folder 52
Merton, Robert K. "Selected Problems Of Field Work In The Planned Community" 1947
Box 18 Folder 53
Mumford, Lewis: "The Plan of New York" 1932; "The Plan of New York: II" 1932; "Regional Planning and the Small Town," Parts I and II; "Cities: Forms and Functions" 1968; "Geddes, Patrick" 1968; press release, 1968; clippings, 1967, 1957, 1950, 1961; "Revaluations I: Howard's Garden City" 1965; "The American Way of Death" 1966; "A Regional Plan To Arrest Megalopolis" 1965; "The Future Of The City" a 5-part series, including: "The Disappearing City" 1962, "Yesterday's City of Comorrow" 1962, "Megalopolis As Anti-City" 1962, "Beginning Of Urban Integration" 1963, "Social Complexity And Urban Design" 1963; "Function And Expression In Architecture" 1951; "The Natural History of Urbanization" 1956; "For Older People -- Not Segregation But Integration" 1956 (included in "Buildings For The Aged" Architectural Record, 1956; "The City As Both Heaven And Hell: a conversation between Graeme Shankland and Lewis Mumford" 1961
Box 18 Folder 54
Mumford, Lewis: "The Human Prospect And Architecture" 1959; "The Highway and the City" 1958; "A New Approach to Workers' Housing" 1957; "The Human Way Out" 1961; "First Annual Purvis Memorial Lecture: The New World Promise" 1965; "A Thought For a Growing South" 1949; "Planning For The Phases Of Life" 1949; "The Social Foundations Of Post-War Building" 1943; "The Plan Of London County" 1945; "The Culture Of Cities, A Review" 1938; "Atom Bond: Miracle or Catastrophe" 1948; "Social Effects" 1947; "The Morals Of Extermination" (no date); "Break The Housing Blockage" 1933; "Mass Production And The Modern House" Parts I and II, 1936; "Regionalism And Irregionalism: The Regional Outlook" 1927; "The Theory And Practice Of Regionalism: From Palotechnic To Neotechnic" 1928; "The Theory And Practice Of Regionalism: The Superiorities Of A Regional City" 1928; "Cities Old And New" Parts I, II, III, 1926
Box 18 Folder 55
Lewis Mumford: Skyline: New Yorker: "Mother Jacob's Home Remedies" 12-1-62; "London and the Laocoon" 11-4-61; "Unesco House I" 11-12-60; "Unesco House II" 11-19-60; "The Skyway's The Limit" 11-14-59; "The Red-Brick Beehives" 5-16-50; "The Roaring Traffic's Boom -- I" 3-18-55; "The Roaring Traffic's Boom -- II" 4-2-55; "The Roaring Traffic's Boom -- III" 4-16-55; "The Roaring Traffic's Boom -- IV" 6-11-55; "More Pelion, More Ossa" 2-3-51; "Babel In Europe" 9-28-57; "The Marseilles 'Folly'" 10-5-57; "The Lesson of the Master" 9-13-58; "Crystal Lantern" 11-13-54; also: "Books: Where The Great City Spreads 3-3-56; and a review by Brendan Gill: "Books: Homage to Mr. Mumford" 8-12-61
